extraction.one_line_summary: JAKOBSEN Fabio won Tour of Guangxi Stage 3 in a sprint finish after breakaway was controlled, taking race lead on bonus seconds.
tactical_patterns: Quick-Step relied entirely on JAKOBSEN to deliver record-breaking win rather than deploying veteran sprinters, revealing confidence in young talent.
Intermediate sprint bonus seconds contested aggressively by breakaway riders DOULL and VAN HOOIDONK rather than gift-wrapped, suggesting tight GC calculations.
GROENEWEGEN's lotto team controlled pace for GC defense but failed to deliver him sprint positioning, exposing team resource allocation weakness.

rider_signals.JAKOBSEN Fabio: positive: Seventh win of rookie season beating established German sprint school riders, now leads overall via bonus seconds
rider_signals.GROENEWEGEN Dylan: negative: Failed to contest podium despite team pacing all day, lost race lead despite starting stage in red
rider_signals.DOULL Owain: neutral: Aggressively hunted intermediate sprint bonuses from breakaway, showing Sky willing to invest diesel domestique energy for marginal GC gains
